page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m4.large is an entry-level instance belonging to the ecs.e server family. It features 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, running on the KVM hypervisor. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, configured with 2 physical cores and a single thread per core. It utilizes a shared CPU allocation model and includes a 33 MB L3 cache. The hardware does not bundle local storage or dedicated GPUs, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tiers. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance is categorized as weak, while multi-core performance falls into the poor category. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached large-block read and write speeds hovering around 2.01 GB/sec and 1.99 GB/sec respectively. These metrics reflect the shared CPU allocation and resource density, which trade raw processing power for qualitative cost efficiency.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the ecs.e-c1m4.large is best suited for lightweight, non-critical workloads. Appropriate use cases include basic web serving, development or staging environments, simple background tasks, and low-traffic applications that do not require sustained high-performance compute or high-speed local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m4.large is an entry-level, general-purpose instance within the ecs.e family. It features 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Operating under a shared CPU allocation model via the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with single-core and multi-core CPU capabilities placing in the lower percentiles, alongside restricted memory bandwidth. Consequently, this instance is highly cost-effective for lightweight workloads that do not demand intensive processing power. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, and low-traffic background tasks.
